_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: Workflow step fails when its agent runtime overflows the model context window

steps.description: Add a nullable max_output_tokens INTEGER column to agent_runtime_options in orch/db/models.py and generate the matching Alembic migration. Backfill known runtimes in the migration: pi/MiniMax-M2.7 -> 131072; claude/opencode rows -> their documented max output where known, otherwise leave NULL. Column mirrors context_window_tokens (CR-00066). Run make migration-check before reporting complete.
QV: Alembic migration round-trip + model drift check

Effective-budget meter. In orch/chat/context_usage.py compute context usage against the EFFECTIVE budget (context_window - max_output_tokens - safety_buffer), not the raw window. Add a function (e.g. compute_effective_context_pct) and read max_output_tokens from agent_runtime_options. NULL max_output_tokens falls back to today's raw-window behaviour. Follow research R-00078.

Per-agent review of S03: effective-budget formula correct (window - max_output - buffer); NULL max_output handled gracefully; reproduction test test_i_00105_context_pct_accounts_for_output_reservation present and behavioural; no scope violation.

Wire the dashboard per-step context gauge (dashboard/routers/items.py + dashboard/templates/fragments/item_steps_table.html) and the chat-assistant gauge (dashboard/routers/chat.py + dashboard/static/chat_assistant/chat.js) to display usage from the effective-budget meter added in S03, so the gauge reflects the usable budget, not the raw window.
Per-agent review of S05: both gauges use the effective-budget meter; no raw-window percentage remains; dashboard tests cover the rendered percentage; no scope violation.

Executor-side tool-output capping with disk spill, compaction-threshold calibration, and context-overflow detection. In executor/step_executor.sh (+ a helper if needed) cap each tool output at a configurable byte budget; on exceed, write the full result to a file under the step work directory and hand the agent a head+tail preview plus the file path (recoverable; NOT in-place truncation). Calibrate proactive compaction to ~75% of the effective budget. Detect a context-window-overflow error in the runtime output and, when the step has not completed cleanly, finalize it as a clean step-fail with a clear blocker naming the overflow instead of letting it limp on (AC4). Config lives in orch/config.py with documented defaults. Follow research R-00078.

Per-agent review of S07: per-tool-output cap is cumulative-aware and config-driven; oversized output is spilled to a file with a recoverable preview+path, never an inline snippet with no spill; compaction threshold calibrated to the effective budget; no docker/migration policy violation; no scope violation.

Reproduction test + regression tests: test_i_00105_context_pct_accounts_for_output_reservation (fails pre-fix, passes post-fix); effective-budget meter across runtimes incl. NULL max_output; the migration applies + backfills; the executor cap helper spills oversized output and passes small output through unchanged. Assert specific values, not shape.

Per-agent review of S09: the reproduction test genuinely fails against pre-fix logic; regression tests assert semantic values (pct >= 100.0, spill file exists with full content), not shape; tests placed in the correct directory; no scope violation.

Global cross-step review of S01..S10: all three ACs verified end-to-end; the migration, meter, gauges, and executor cap are mutually consistent; scope integrity (git diff confined to scope.allowed_paths); make migration-check still green; no production-policy violation. Runs the full unit + integration suites.
QV: Linting
QV: Assertion scanner
QV: Formatting
QV: Type checking
QV: Unit tests
QV: Integration tests
QV: Diff coverage
QV: Secret scanning (gitleaks)
Self-assessment of the just-completed item via the iw-item-analyze skill.
